back_translation: Let the love-of-brothers continue to remain.
Do not neglect to show kindness in receiving strangers, because by doing this, some have welcomed angels without realizing it.
Remember those who are imprisoned, as if you were imprisoned together with them; and those who are mistreated, as if you yourselves are also bodily.
Let marriage be honored among everyone, and let the marriage bed be without defilement; for God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterers.
Let your way of life be free from love of money; be content with what you have; for He Himself has spoken: 'I will never abandon you, and I will never forsake you absolutely.'
Therefore we say with confidence: 'The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?'
Remember your leaders, those who spoke to you the word of God; consider the outcome of their way of life, and imitate their faith.
Jesus Christ remains the same — yesterday, today, and forever.
Do not be carried away by various and strange teachings; for it is good that your hearts be strengthened by grace, not by foods, by which those who follow them have not received benefit.
We have an altar from which those who serve in the tabernacle have no right to eat.
For the blood of animals which is brought into the Holy Place by the high priest as a sin offering — the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
Therefore Jesus also suffered outside the city gate, in order to sanctify the people through His own blood.
Therefore let us go out to Him outside the camp, bearing His reproach.
For here we do not have a city that abides forever, but we are seeking the city that is to come.
Therefore, through Him, let us continually offer a sacrifice of praise to God, namely the fruit of lips that confess His name.
And do not neglect to do what is good and to share together; because God is well-pleased with sacrifices like these.

Obey your leaders, and submit to them; because they keep watch over your souls as those who must give an account; cause them to do this with joy, not with groaning, because that groaning will be of no benefit to you.
Pray for us, because we are confident that we have a good conscience, and have the desire to live with honor in everything.
And I urgently plead with you to do this, so that I may return to you soon.
May the God of peace, who brought our Lord Jesus, who is the great Shepherd of the sheep, out from the dead through the blood of the eternal covenant,
may He equip you in everything good, in order to do His will, and may He do in us that which is well-pleasing before Him through Jesus Christ; may glory be to Him forever and ever. Amen.
Brothers and sisters, I plead with you to bear with this word of encouragement, because I have written to you only briefly.
Let it be known to you that Timothy our brother has been released; if Timothy arrives soon, I will come together with Timothy to see you.
Send greetings to all your leaders and all the holy ones. Those who come from Italy also send greetings to you.
May grace remain with all of you.
